behavior_in_task_description.reason: Every test-asserted specific — function names (resolveAuthorForCommit, appendAuthors, resolveUsername), variable names (authorCache, commitMatch, bumpedPackages, changelogPath, versionHeader), literal strings ('No changelog entries', 'CHANGELOG.md', '#### ${pkg.name}'), regex patterns ([a-f0-9]), version comparison expression (prevPkg.version !== currentPkg.version), imports (fs, path, execSync, globSync), token names (GH_TOKEN, GITHUB_TOKEN), and file path (scripts/create-github-release.mjs) —

no_solution_leakage.reason: instruction.md dictates exact implementation details: specific variable names (authorCache, commitMatch, bumpedPackages, changelogPath, versionHeader), exact function signatures (async function resolveAuthorForCommit, async function appendAuthors), exact expressions (prevPkg.version !== currentPkg.version), exact header format (#### ${pkg.name}), and exact fallback string ('No changelog entries'). This leaves virtually no reasoning about WHAT to change — it's a specification of the exact code to

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on gold-specific variable names (authorCache, commitMatch, bumpedPackages, changelogPath, versionHeader), exact function names (resolveAuthorForCommit, appendAuthors), exact regex pattern '[a-f0-9]', exact expression 'prevPkg.version !== currentPkg.version', and exact header format '#### ${pkg.name}'. An alternative correct implementation using different variable names (e.g., 'cachedAuthors', 'hashPattern', 'changedPackages') or different comparison logic would fail these tests.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Almost every fail-to-pass test is purely textual: they read the script file and assert on string presence (e.g., 'assert "async function resolveAuthorForCommit" in content', 'assert "authorCache" in content', 'assert "No changelog entries" in content'). Only test_script_is_valid_esm and test_eslint_passes actually execute the code. No test imports/calls the functions or verifies runtime behavior.

test_not_tautological.reason: All fail-to-pass tests check for string presence in the file content. An agent could write a stub file containing all the required strings as comments or dead code (e.g., '// async function resolveAuthorForCommit', '// authorCache', '// No changelog entries') and pass every test without implementing the actual bug fix.

behavior_in_tests.reason: instruction.md requires that the script actually reads CHANGELOG.md files, extracts version sections, resolves authors via GitHub API, and generates correct release notes. None of this runtime behavior is tested — tests only verify string presence in the source file.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized into 'FAIL-TO-PASS' and 'PASS-TO-PASS' sections with clear comments, descriptive function names, and docstrings explaining each test's purpose.

structured_data_schema.reason: The task produces a script that generates markdown text, not structured data (JSON/CSV/API/DB).

no_typos.reason: All filenames, paths, variable names, and commands in instruction.md match those referenced in tests without typos.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: instruction.md specifies exact variable names (authorCache, commitMatch, bumpedPackages, changelogPath, versionHeader), exact function signatures, exact comparison expressions, and exact string literals. This goes far beyond describing the problem — it prescribes the implementation at a line-level detail.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task modifying a single script file, not a config edit task.
